reviews.review_description:
There's not much to this game: you aim to jump higher. There's an added element of focusing on your phone like a narcissistic social media obsessed child. Also, your character is a gym bro. 

If you like a challenge and want to compete with others, that alone will keep you coming back. Besides that ocd drive, there's not much else to this game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing! While jumping higher is definitely the core challenge, there’s actually quite a bit more under the hood. Every few hundred meters you hit Gyms where you can grab upgrades, take on wild tasks that make your run harder for bonus points, or get Bro Buffs for temporary boosts.

There are also achievements, leaderboards, hidden events, and even the Underbulk. The goal is to keep improving your stats and see how far you can climb, both literally and on the boards!

Appreciate you checking it out and pushing for those gains!

reviews.review_description: This is one unusual game, but crazy fun. You bounce from block to block until you reach the end and get the chance to upgrade your buff dude and try out ridiculous downgrades for more of a challenge. The longer you go, the more obstacles get in your way, making it even harder. It’s so simple but does it perfectly. Even when you fail, it makes you want to jump right back in. It’s addictive, goofy, and absolutely worth playing. And seriously, don't look up because it might make you super dizzy.

reviews.review_title: Okay concept but not my cup of tea.
reviews.review_description: The game play mechanics makes sense and can be enjoyable at times but this game is probably not designed for me. The gym bro element got tiring pretty fast.

reviews.review_title: Feels like I'm streaming! Very Fun!
reviews.review_description:
This unique title blends platforming challenges with a clever streaming-themed twist. You’re not just trying to conquer increasingly difficult levels; you’re also “streaming” to an in-game audience and interacting with a lively chat as you play.

As someone who creates content myself, I was surprised by how naturally the game captures the feeling of streaming live, juggling gameplay, chat engagement, and the pressure to perform. It doubles as great practice for anyone thinking of getting into streaming, helping you build awareness and multitasking skills in a fun environment.

The platforming itself is solid, the progression keeps you on your toes, and the constant interaction layer makes the whole experience feel fresh and replayable. It’s definitely different from the games I usually play, but that’s exactly why it stood out. I had a genuinely enjoyable time with it, and I can easily see people coming back for multiple runs just to improve their flow and keep their “audience” entertained.

Overall, a creative and surprisingly engaging mix of gameplay and streamer-style interaction.

This is basically Doodle Jump VR if Doodle took tren and hit the gym. Each level has its own set of challenges, and you can improve your gym bro each time you complete one. It’s suprisingly smooth, where I didn’t notice any motion sickness despite the core of the gameplay being bouncing around. Wish I could pick up the weights at the menu and the zones in between levels! 

Overall this is an entertaining game and simple enough for all kinds of players.

Jacked Up is a fun and original VR platformer built around bouncing your way through tricky obstacles to reach the next gym. The movement feels precise and satisfying, making every leap and dodge count.

Along the way, you unlock and embody new avatars like frogs or pigs, which adds charm and variety. The thrill of finally reaching a new gym—and meeting new characters—makes progression rewarding and keeps you pushing forward.

Currently in early access, the developers are inviting community ideas, which is always exciting for shaping the future of the game. A clever, well-executed VR experience that’s both challenging and lighthearted. Definitely worth a try.

reviews.review_title: Such a fun and silly game!
reviews.review_description:
In Jacked Up, you're a buff dude (?) bouncing up platforms, doing your best not to fall down. 
Such a simple premise, and such addictive gameplay loop! 

From time to time, you'll have the chance to upgrade your character, helping you in your quest to reach the top, if it's even possible. 

In my opinion, this is a great intro to VR due to the simple controls, yet encompassing experience.

Now if you'll excuse me, I have more miles to jump before I fall to my doom!

To say Jacked Up is a deep game would be wrong. In fact the sky beneath you  would indicate otherwise, but it's a strong and simple loop tasking you to flex to your viewers and go upwards, accepting optional challenges along the way. As if that wasn't a good enough time, the option to record yourself and speak to your audience let me do the meathead gymbro roleplay of my dreams. 

If you want a fun and simple game to flex on, I ABSOLUTELY recommend Jacked Up!
